info.name: Adaptive Difficulty Engine API
info.description:
Complete API collection for testing the Adaptive Difficulty & Anti-Cheat Engine.

WORKFLOW:
1. Run 'Start Session' first
2. Copy the session_token from the response
3. Update the {{session_token}} variable
4. Get a question, submit an answer, check security report

variable.description: Change this if running on a different host/port
Copy from Get Next Question response

request.description:
FIRST STEP: Start a new quiz session.
Copy the session_token from the response!

Get the next question for your session.
Replace {{session_token}} with your actual token.
Get a question filtered by topic.

body.raw:
{
  "session_token": "{{session_token}}",
  "question_id": {{question_id}},
  "answer_text": "A variable is a named storage location in memory that holds a value which can change during program execution. Variables are used to store data that can be referenced and manipulated throughout a program.",
  "time_taken_seconds": 45.0,
  "paste_detected": false
}

request.description:
Submit a good answer at normal speed.
Expected: Low risk score, decent similarity.

body.raw:
{
  "session_token": "{{session_token}}",
  "question_id": {{question_id}},
  "answer_text": "A variable is a named storage location in memory that holds a value which can change during program execution. Variables have a name, type, and value.",
  "time_taken_seconds": 0.3,
  "paste_detected": true
}

request.description:
Submit with suspicious timing + paste detected.
Expected: High risk score, multiple flags!
View the full anti-cheat report for the session.
See current difficulty level and performance trend.
See all answers submitted in this session.
Add a new question to the system.
Check if Redis is connected or using memory fallback.
